repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

origination.label: creator
origination.persname: Goldstaub, Paul R., 1947-2014 (Title of work: Venetian echoes..)

origination.label: creator
origination.persname: Goldstaub, Sylvia. (Title of work: Unconditional love..)

did.physdesc: Papers, photographs, correspondence, posters, videotapes, audiotapes, 8mm film reels, CDs, musical score, press releases, ephemera, items of clothing, birth and death certificates, scrapbooks, autobiographical essay.
did.abstract: The Goldstaub family papers relate to the effect on the family of Sylvia and Bernie Goldstaub when their son Mark came out to them as a gay man in 1977 and later when he became HIV-positive. The collection includes an audiotape of "Venetian Echoes" composed by Paul Goldstaub in memory of Mark, as well as the score and program notes. Also materials relating to Sylvia's book Unconditional Love: Mom! Dad! Love Me Please!

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

defitem.label: EAD encoding:
defitem.item: Mireille Lee, March 2000Sarah Keen, July 2008Sarah Keen, January 2009

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Goldstaub Family papers, #7567. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Mark Goldstaub was born April 24, 1951, and died from an AIDS-related illness December 14, 1988. He worked as a publicist in New York City for entertainers including Shirley MacLaine. He was survived by his parents, Sylvia and Bernie Goldstaub, his brother Paul Goldstaub, and his partner, Edmund Wojcik. Motivated to share her family's story and to persuade other parents to continue to love their gay children, Sylvia Goldstaub wrote a book in Mark.s honor, Unconditional Love: Mom! Dad! Love Me Please! Sylvia has toured the country speaking about her book and her experiences at book stores, religious groups, and AIDS fund-raising events. Sylvia and Bernie were active members of Parents and Friends of Lesbians and Gays in Florida. Bernie died in 2003. Paul Goldstaub, a professor of music, composed "Venetian Echoes" as a tribute to Mark.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: The Goldstaub family papers document the lives of Sylvia and Bernie Goldstaub and their sons Paul and Mark. They include family photographs, correspondence, and 12 reels of 8mm home movies, as well as extensive documentation of the whole family's experience with Mark Goldstaub's illnesses and death from AIDS-related complications. Sylvia's tireless advocacy and AIDS education work is reflected in videotaped interviews and lectures; the red ribbon-studded outfit she wore on her publicity tour for the book Unconditional Love: Mom! Dad! Love Me Please!, and letters of support from people and organizations in response to her book. Also included are correspondence and condolences to the family; audiotapes of interviews with Sylvia and Bernie Goldstaub; a folder of material on PFLAG; press releases; biographical information for the family; ephemera collected by Sylvia Goldstaub relating to AIDS activism; a file on the lobbying effort AIDS Watch '93; an audiotape, the score, and program notes for "Venetian Echoes" composed by Paul Goldstaub in memory of Mark, and Paul's 1998 CD "I am Prospero" that includes "Venetian Echoes." The collection also includes personal material of Mark Goldstaub: birth and death certificates; childhood scrapbooks and an autobiographical essay; a 1981-1983 photo album; a crew jacket for Shirley MacLaine's 1984 Broadway show, for which Mark was publicist; posters and other material relating to his work and experience with AIDS; a program for his memorial service; and correspondence of the family of Mark's partner, Edmund Wojcik.

Publicity for Sylvia Goldstaub's lecture at Temple Beth El Zedeck on WISH-TV 8 News, Indianapolis, Indiana, October 14, 1991. Includes a short clip of Mark Goldstaub's panel in the NAMES Project quilt. Lecture is with Dr. Martin Kleiman, Sylvia Goldstaub's cousin and doctor to Ryan White

USE COPY of: Publicity for Sylvia Goldstaub's lecture at Temple Beth El Zedeck on WISH-TV 8 News, Indianapolis, Indiana, October 14, 1991. Includes a short clip of Mark Goldstaub's panel in the NAMES Project quilt. Lecture is with Dr. Martin Kleiman, Sylvia Goldstaub's cousin and doctor to Ryan White

Broadcast about PFLAG (Parents and Friends of Lesbians and Gays) hosted by Sheldon Katz with Sylvia and Bernie Goldstaub. The Goldstaub's discuss their involvement in the organization and the death of their son Mark from AIDS.
Sylvia Goldstaub discusses her experience as a mother of a gay son who died of AIDS. Vail, Colorado, March 13, 1993
USE COPY of: Sylvia Goldstaub discusses her experience as a mother of a gay son who died of AIDS. Vail, Colorado, March 13, 1993

"Paths to Spirituality: AIDS, Homosexuality and Unconditional Love - An Interview with Sylvia and Bernie Goldstaub," Sylvia Goldstaub discusses her experience as a mother of a gay son who died of AIDS. [sound is muffled]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: July 1951- Paul's 4th birthday, Mark 3 months old thru 1955 Sylvia and Bernie's 10th Anniversary

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1955 Spring school play (Paul), Miss Tinnan 2nd grade; 1955 Cabana Club July; 1955 Christmas; 1956 3rd grade (Paul) Mrs. Silva March 14th; April 1956 Mark's 5th birthday; 1956 Cabana Club July; Sept. 1956 Mark's 1st day of Kindergarten; 1957 July Paul's 10th birthday at the Cabana Club; 1957 Sept. school opens, Yom Kippur

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

First day school- Paul's first scout hike Oct. 1958; 1959 Mar. Paul's 6th grade play and magic(?); 1959 June Mark's 2nd grade play; 1960 Apr. new home; May 1960 Memorial Day

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Paul's Bar Mitzvah, June 4 1960, Irvington Home Irvington, NJ

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: June 19 1960 Sylvia and Bernie 15th anniversary; Sept.1960 1st day school to Mark's 10th birthday April 24 1961; 1961 July Cape Cod; 1961 Sept. First day school; 1961 Dec. Ice skating; 1962 June 1, Feb. 2

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1963 First day school and football game; 1964 Decoration for May parade; 1964 Summer and Niagara Falls; 1964 Sept. Paul senior Mark 8th grade and Jr. High; 1965 June Paul graduation from Columbia HS; 1965 Sept. Paul freshman Ithaca College; 1965 Mark freshman Jr. High(?); 1966 Spring Ithaca College; 1966 Mark sophomore Columbia HS; 1966 Paul sophomore Ithaca College

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1967 March Paul (Jr.) Columbia HS; 1967 Paul (Jr.) Ithaca College; 1968 Sept. Paul (Sr.) Horse Head practice; 1968 Sylvia/Mark Senior Columbia HS; 1969 May Paul graduates Ithaca College; 1969 June Mark graduates Columbia HS and prom; 1969 Sept. Mark freshman Ithaca College; 1970 Aug. Mark and Laura sophomores at Ithaca College
